subject: A rounded stone specimen with multiple fossil impressions embedded in its surface — visible shapes include a crab or trilobite-like form, a spiral shell, and a fish-like silhouette, all rendered in the stone
description: A smooth, rounded gray stone rests at the center of the frame. Its surface contains several distinct fossil impressions: a crab or trilobite-like arthropod shape on the left side, a coiled spiral shell near the top, and what appears to be a small fish or elongated creature toward the right. The fossils are rendered in muted earth tones — tan, orange-brown, and teal-green — against the pale stone. The background is soft and diffuse, fading from light blue-purple at the edges to near-white at center. The overall impression is of a specimen laid out for display, lit from above with gentle ambient light.
foreground: rounded gray stone with embedded fossil impressions
background: soft diffuse gradient, light blue-purple fading to near-white
emotion: neutral — object card, no figure present
